claim_boundary: BHSM v1.3F clarifies that internal modes and virtual dressing contributions are not automatically new observable particles. Extra observable light states remain forbidden unless identified experimentally or lifted/screened by the H_T/scalar-sector mechanisms.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R1: accepted on-shell SM field state.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R2: QCD bound states are composite, not elementary BHSM ledger entries.

rationale: R2: QCD bound states are composite, not elementary BHSM ledger entries.

rationale: R2: QCD bound states are composite, not elementary BHSM ledger entries.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R3: internal Berger-Hopf modes are not automatically particles.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R6: unrecognized light observable internal state is forbidden.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R7: internal complement mode is above the Hopf/H_T lift scale.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R8: scalar/topographic mode is Higgs-projected or screened.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R4: off-shell temporary contributions are virtual excitations unless on-shell.

limitations: State ontology is semantic; it does not change model predictions.
rationale: R5: virtual-environment corrections are dressing contributions, not particles.

limitations: This ontology does not derive the SM field ledger.
rules.statement: Standard Model elementary particles are real/on-shell particle states only if they correspond to the accepted SM field ledger.

limitations: Composite classification is semantic and does not solve QCD confinement.
rules.statement: Composite hadrons, mesons, baryons, resonances, and bound states are downstream QCD composite states, not elementary BHSM ledger entries.

limitations: The full internal spectrum remains under H_T audit.
rules.statement: Internal Berger-Hopf eigenmodes are not automatically observable particles.

limitations: Loop/dressing language is an ontology distinction, not a new numerical prediction.
rules.statement: Virtual charm, top, quark, lepton, gauge, or scalar contributions are virtual excitations or dressing contributions unless they correspond to on-shell states.

limitations: The dressed branch remains a candidate branch, not canonical adoption.
rules.statement: Virtual-environment corrections such as Z_virt^{u,2}=1/2 are dressing contributions, not new particles.

limitations: The full H_T no-extra-light theorem remains scaffolded, not proven.
rules.statement: Any additional internal mode below the H_T gap that couples as an observable on-shell state is a forbidden extra-light state unless experimentally identified.

limitations: The full H_T spectrum is still open.
rules.statement: Heavy internal modes above the Hopf/H_T lift scale are heavy lifted states.

limitations: Full scalar/topographic decoupling from the action remains open.
rules.statement: Scalar/topographic modes are allowed only if Higgs-projected, heavy, derivative-filtered, curvature-filtered, or screened; otherwise they are forbidden extra-light states.

states.description: Accepted on-shell Standard Model charged-lepton field state.
states.name: electron

states.description: Accepted SM charm field state when treated as a real field excitation.
states.name: charm quark field excitation

states.description: Accepted on-shell SM gauge field state.
states.name: photon

states.description: Berger-Hopf internal mode used in the charged-lepton ledger.
states.name: charged-lepton internal mode (5,2)
notes: Internal mode label is not automatically an on-shell particle.

states.description: Hypothetical light internal mode below the H_T gap that couples as an observable on-shell state.
states.name: non-SM light internal mode below H_T gap

states.description: Internal complement mode lifted above the Hopf/H_T scale.
states.name: heavy internal complement mode above 4 pi^2 v

states.description: Scalar/topographic mode allowed through screening rather than as an extra light direct-coupled state.
states.name: screened topographic scalar mode

states.description: Temporary charm-sector contribution treated as virtual/off-shell or dressing structure.
states.name: temporary charm-sector loop/dressing effect

states.description: Virtual-environment dressing contribution for the pure-fiber middle-up mode.
states.name: Z_virt^{u,2}=1/2
notes: Dressing contribution changes only c/t in the dressed candidate branch.

limitations: This is a semantic and structural ontology layer, not a model-output change.
It does not compute the full H_T spectrum or prove the no-extra-light theorem.
title: BHSM v1.3F State Ontology and Particle/Mode Classification Ledger
